items.title: Police reveal former lecturer scammed of RM749,000 after suspects claimed money laundering link
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, May 21 — A former lecturer lost RM749,000 after she was deceived by a phone scam last year, Kuala Terengganu district police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said. He added that the victim was contacted on July 13, 2025 by suspects impersonating insurance agents and the police, who accused her of being involved in money laundering and had to pay a sum of money for a Bank Negara investigation. She was also instructed to transfer money to several different accounts they provided.

items.title: US denies triggering Norway’s export halt, says it helped integrate missile components for Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — The United States has denied any involvement in Norway’s decision to revoke export approval for a naval strike missile system ordered by Malaysia. The clarification came after reports by Free Malaysia Today and other outlets highlighted Norway’s move to cancel the export permit for the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system supplied by Kongsberg Defence & Aerospace under a 2018 contract. US embassy chargé d’affaires David H Gamble Jr said Washington supported Malaysia’s efforts to strengthen its maritime defence, adding that the US backed Malaysia’s acquisition of a naval strike capability to respond to regional threats and to protect its sovereignty.

items.title: Some Ukrainian refugees head home - for dental work
items.summary: FOUR years of brutal Russian attacks have forced millions of Ukrainians to seek safety abroad. But with the war showing no signs of stopping, significant numbers of refugees have started trickling back home, if only temporarily, for one reason: to see their dentist. Around 90 per cent, the 39-year-old said, are Ukrainians.

items.title: Teen pleads guilty to kicking 10-year-old in the face, fracturing his jaw over fallen bicycle
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A Form Five student pleaded guilty in the magistrate's court here today to causing grievous hurt to a Year Four pupil by kicking him in the face and fracturing his jaw last Friday. The 17-year-old accused entered the plea after the charge under Section 325 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing grievous hurt, was read out before magistrate Arun Noval Dass. According to the charge, he was accused of kicking the 10-year-old boy's jaw and causing injuries at a playground in Tongkang Pechah here at 9pm on May 15.

items.title: Services, F&B SMEs top list of Perkeso non-compliance
items.summary: He said among the common reasons given by employers were a lack of awareness of the obligation to register workers with Perkeso, as well as attempts to reduce operating costs. "Perkeso has existed for almost 60 years. He said the situation proved that many employers still took workers' social protection lightly.

items.title: EC gazettes April voter roll, with 39,111 Malaysians automatically registered under Undi18
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 21 — A total of 39,111 citizens aged 18 and above between April 1 and 30 were automatically registered as new voters under the April 2026 Supplementary Electoral Roll (DPT BLN4/2026). Election Commission (EC) secretary Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus said the supplementary electoral roll, which was verified on May 20 and gazetted today, also contained 15,880 registered voters who changed constituencies and 1,369 voters who changed voter categories or status. Khairul Shahril said voters whose names are not listed in DPT BLN4/2026 may submit claims by filling Form C through the EC online application or at the relevant state election office.

items.title: Hospitalised Thai princess's condition 'deteriorated'
items.summary: BANGKOK: The health condition of a Thai princess who has been hospitalised for more than three years has deteriorated, the palace said Thursday. Princess Bajrakitiyabha Mahidol, the king's eldest daughter, fell ill during a military dog training session in December 2022 and has remained in hospital since. She suffered from a severe blood infection and her lung and kidney functions were being supported by medical devices and medication, the palace previously said.

items.title: Illegal Jeli gold panning site shut before fatal landslide
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The authorities had sealed off a stream near the Jerimbong oil palm plantation in Jeli yesterday, before two men were later buried in a landslide while allegedly carrying out illegal gold mining activities. A source said the area was believed to contain large gold deposits and had attracted many locals over the past few months. "However, shortly after the operation, several locals, including the three men involved in the incident, are believed to have returned to the stream to continue their gold mining activities," the source said.

items.title: Psychiatrist: Parents, peers given greater weight in Zara's psychological autopsy report
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Testimonies from the late Zara Qairina Mahathir's extended family members carried less weight in the psychological autopsy report compared with those of her parents and peers, the Coroner's Court heard today. Consultant forensic psychiatrist Dr Chua Sze Hung testified that while interviewing multiple informants was crucial, the selection of interviewees needed to be tailored individually for adolescents. Dr Chua said he decided that interviews with Zara Qairina's mother and father were sufficient to examine her upbringing and her relationship with her parents.

items.title: Zeti calls for more investment in education and skills
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Bank Negara Malaysia governor Tan Sri Dr Zeti Akhtar Aziz has called for greater investment in talent development and education, cautioning that Malaysia's future competitiveness will depend on how well it prepares its workforce for a rapidly changing global economy. "The investment in education is a vital infrastructure investment that will determine our future growth potential," she said. She added that a more skilled workforce would help Malaysia improve productivity, create new opportunities and strengthen the country's competitiveness.

items.title: A-G opposes Malaysian Bar's challenge against Zahid's NFA status
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Attorney-General (A-G) is opposing the Malaysian Bar's bid to challenge the decision to classify Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi's Yayasan Akalbudi case as No Further Action (NFA). Senior federal counsel Nurhafizza Azizan, appearing for the A-G, told the High Court during case management of the Bar's leave application to commence judicial review proceedings before Judge Alice Loke Yee Ching today. The Bar is seeking a court order to compel the Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC) to recharge Zahid over all 47 charges of criminal breach of trust, corruption and money laundering involving Yayasan Akalbudi funds.

items.title: Late teen's diaries provide more material than family recordings, psychiatrist tells Zara Qairina inquest
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The diaries of the late 13-year-old Zara Qairina Mahathir provide more material evidence of her internal mental state than audio recordings or verbal interactions with family members, the Coroner's Court heard today. Consultant forensic psychiatrist Dr Chua Sze Hung testified that verbal interactions alone are a less accurate indicator of an individual's psychological well-being. The diary would be more material," the 67th witness testified before coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan.

items.title: 2025 records highest Perkeso prosecutions and compounds in five years
items.summary: Perkeso group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed said 5,099 compound cases and 4,536 prosecution cases had been recorded this year, surpassing the 2024 figures of 4,394 compounds and 3,677 prosecutions. He said data as of April showed there were 915,616 active registered employers nationwide, but only 74 per cent were actively contributing for their workers, leaving 238,060 employers, or 26 per cent, non-compliant. He said this year's enforcement would focus particularly on employers hiring foreign workers, especially in cases involving outsourced labour through employment agencies.

items.title: PM Anwar pledges commitment to secure release of Gaza flotilla activists, says SNCC
items.summary: SEPANG, May 21 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has given his assurance and full commitment to lead efforts through bilateral ties to secure the immediate release of Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 humanitarian activists detained by Israel. Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) director-general Datuk Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said the commitment was conveyed by the prime minister to SNCC during a telephone conversation today. “They also condemned the interception and detention of humanitarian flotilla activists in international waters,” he said.

items.title: MCMC issues statutory demand to TikTok over offensive content
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has issued a statutory demand to TikTok over the platform's failure to act promptly and adequately against offensive and defamatory content, as well as fake accounts targeting the royal institution. "The content in question includes material that is grossly offensive, false, menacing and insulting in nature, including AI-generated videos and manipulated images, which may contravene Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, as well as other laws in Malaysia. MCMC said the statutory demand requires TikTok to undertake immediate remedial measures, including strengthening its content moderation mechanisms, ensuring more effective enforcement against content that violates Malaysian laws and community standards, and providing a formal explanation regarding its moderation failures.

items.title: Mother of 3 among trio pleads guilty to robbery, stripping of woman
items.summary: Nur Syuhaidah Saharan, 34; Aliza Khamis, 45; and Mohamad Salehuddin Ali, 34, were named as those involved. Salehuddin was charged with four offences, Nur Syuhaidah with three, and Aliza with one. They were charged under Section 340 of the Penal Code for wrongful confinement, punishable under Section 342, read with Section 34 for common intention.

items.title: Tengku Zafrul thanks Selangor Sultan for Aidiladha reminder on unity and Rukun Negara
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — Senior political advisor to the Prime Minister Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz has expressed his gratitude to the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, for granting him an audience yesterday and for His Royal Highness’ reminder on the importance of unity. In a Facebook post, Tengku Zafrul said the Sultan’s advice should be understood within the framework of Malaysia’s constitutional monarchy, which has long been a pillar of national stability. Tengku Zafrul stressed that the Sultan’s call was aimed at strengthening unity across Malaysia’s diverse society, not solely among Malays, and highlighted how Malay and Islamic values should serve as a foundation for national stability that protects all communities.

items.title: Perkeso gives grace period until June 22 for non-compliant employers
items.summary: Perkeso group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed said the initiative was introduced after enforcement data showed employer non-compliance remained high at about 26 per cent. Mohammed Azman said findings from Op Kesan also showed that between 19 and 21 per cent of workers checked over the years were still not registered with Perkeso. Mohammed Azman said 495,380 foreign workers registered with Perkeso since 2019 had no contribution records.

items.title: Foreign scam syndicates exploiting tourist passes to enter Malaysia, says police
items.summary: Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said investigations revealed that several foreign suspects arrested in recent raids in Sepang had entered the country as early as the beginning of the year, March, and April. He said police are conducting cross-checks with the Immigration Department to verify the travel documents and immigration status of all suspects involved. Shazeli said three separate operations carried out by the Sepang district commercial crime investigation department successfully crippled several foreign-linked syndicates involved in online investment scams and financial data collection activities.

items.title: Retiree, 75, pleads not guilty to reckless driving charge in Klang crash that killed two sisters
items.summary: KLANG, May 21 — A 75-year-old man pleaded not guilty at the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of reckless driving that caused the deaths of two sisters, last Wednesday. Ang Kim Lai made the plea after the charge was read to him in Mandarin before Magistrate A. Khartiyayini. According to the charge, Ang, a private sector retiree, who was driving a sport utility vehicle (SUV), was accused of driving recklessly and causing the deaths of Wong Nyok Sim, 62, and Wong Nyoke Fong, 57, at Jalan Pekan Baru here at 7.30pm on May 15.

items.title: Japan-Malaysia partnership matters more than ever
items.summary: Japan has already begun collaborating with Malaysia in non-military spheres too, addressing China's pressure campaign on rare earth supply chains. In February, Tokyo embarked on its first technical cooperation project with Putrajaya focused on critical mineral development, reflecting a vital understanding that economic resilience and strategic autonomy are deeply intertwined. Last year, the Chinese Coast Guard (CCG) spent 261 calendar days in the northeastern area of Malaysia's Exclusive Economic Zone.

items.title: Speculation mounts over possible early Johor state election
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Speculation is mounting that Johor could be heading towards an early state election following talk of a special state assembly sitting on June 24, with political circles claiming invitations have already been circulated. The rumours have intensified whispers that the special sitting could be linked to a possible dissolution of the state assembly to pave the way for fresh state polls. "There is a strong possibility that if a special state assembly sitting is held on June 24, the agenda or proceedings may involve the dissolution of the Johor State Assembly," he said.

items.title: Turkey passes law to halve corporate tax for manufacturing companies
items.summary: ANKARA: Turkey's parliament passed a law that cuts corporate tax for manufacturing companies, introduces incentives to repatriate assets held abroad and extends tax advantages for financial services exports, according to the legislation. Under the law, corporate tax for manufacturing companies will be reduced to 12.5 per cent, from the current 25 per cent. The law extends a 100 per cent corporate tax exemption on financial services export income at the Istanbul Financial Centre, a hub for banks and financial companies in central Istanbul, until 2047.

items.title: Ministry steps up financing aid amid Middle East tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry has rolled out a range of financing support measures to help industries cope with the ongoing global supply crisis and geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Its deputy minister Sim Tze Tzin said the assistance, particularly targeted at SMEs, is aimed at easing cash flow pressures and supporting the shift towards alternative supply chains and energy sources. Under the scheme, SMEs can obtain financing at a two per cent interest rate, while non-SMEs are eligible for financing at five per cent interest.

items.title: Boys sexually abused at Gombak welfare home now 'a shell of their former selves', says family
items.summary: The victims' family members said the children are finding it hard to come to terms with what happened to them. The mother of one of the boys said her son had become withdrawn and fearful following the abuse. Judge Norshila Kamaruddin meted out the sentence to 25-year-old Retna Velu , who pleaded guilty to six sexual offences involving children.

items.title: Govt aims to table Senior Citizens Bill in next Parliament session
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government targets to table the Senior Citizens Bill in the next Parliament session, subject to procedural coordination and further review by the Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC). Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the draft bill has been prepared, but stakeholders are still being consulted to ensure diverse views are taken into consideration before it is finalised. "The bill is expected to include provisions on parental care responsibilities and the welfare of senior citizens to address the country's growing ageing population.

items.title: Sarawak ramps up business events with infrastructure push, aviation connectivity
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sarawak is strengthening its position as a regional business events hub through strategic investments in infrastructure, aviation connectivity and industry partnerships, as it shifts from destination marketing towards long-term economic impact and sector development. The state is banking on large-scale infrastructure upgrades and ecosystem development to capture greater opportunities in the global meetings, incentives, conventions and exhibitions industry, particularly as business travel and international conferences continue to recover post-pandemic. Hii Chang Kee, chairman of Business Events Sarawak (BESarawak), said the state's international engagement is increasingly focused on attracting collaborations that generate broader economic benefits rather than merely promoting Sarawak as an event venue.

items.title: Penang says food supply sufficient ahead of Aidiladha, launches local vegetable campaign
items.summary: "Supplies of the 27 controlled items remain sufficient and prices are still below the ceiling level," he said after launching a campaign promoting locally grown vegetables at Komtar. Meanwhile, state Agrotechnology, Food Security and Cooperative Development Committee chairman Datuk Rashidi Zinol said local vegetable growers had been struggling with low farmgate prices despite higher retail prices. Farmers have faced difficulties because farmgate prices had fallen too low," he said.

items.title: Amirudin lauds Selangor Sultan’s Aidiladha message urging Malaysians to defend unity, uphold Rukun Negara
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari today expressed his gratitude for the Hari Raya Aidiladha 2026 address by the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah. He said the Sultan’s message highlighted the importance of unity, harmony in Malaysia’s multiracial society, and the duty of all citizens to safeguard peace and stability in Selangor and nationwide. Amirudin noted the royal address came at a time when some quarters were exploiting racial and religious sentiments and politicising the monarchy for personal gain.

items.title: 'Add' call on MR DIY following better 1Q 2026 results – CGS International
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: CGS International Securities Malaysia Sdn Bhd has reiterated its 'Add' call for MR DIY Group (M) Bhd after the company posted improved results in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (1Q 2026). In a research note today, it said CGS International remains positive on MR DIY's growth trajectory. "MR DIY currently trades at a 2027 forecast price-to-earnings ratio of 19.4 times.

items.title: FMM calls for gazettement of Government Procurement Act without delay
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Federation of Malaysian Manufacturing (FMM) has called for the immediate enforcement of the Government Procurement Act (GPA) 2025, citing global supply chain disruptions and rising cost pressures linked to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. He said the GPA 2025 represents Malaysia's first dedicated legislation governing public procurement, with implementation currently pending the appointment of a commencement date by the Finance Minister through a Federal Gazette notification. Lee said FMM had long advocated for a single procurement law to consolidate various treasury instructions, circulars and financial regulations that previously governed public procurement, which lacked consistent statutory enforcement.

items.title: China's Xi may visit North Korea as early as next week, Yonhap reports
items.summary: SEOUL: Chinese President Xi Jinping may visit North Korea as early as next week and will try to act as a mediator between North Korean leader Kim Jong Un and U.S. President Donald Trump, South Korea's Yonhap news agency reported, quoting a senior government official. Xi, who hosted Trump last week, will try to act as a mediator between Trump and North Korea's Kim, the senior official was quoted as saying in the report released late on Wednesday. North Korean leader Kim visited Beijing last year and stood alongside Xi and Russian President Vladimir Putin at a major military parade.

items.title: Restaurant chain director charged with RM1.4 million CBT
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A director of a steamboat restaurant chain pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to criminal breach of trust involving more than RM1.4 million in company funds two years ago. Deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Zul Hilmi Latiff proposed bail at RM120,000 in one surety. However, defence counsel Lee Keng Fatt appealed for a lower bail of RM50,000, saying his client is the family's sole breadwinner and has three school-going children.

items.title: Debt collector to be charged over alleged sexual assault of teenager
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A debt collector arrested for allegedly confining, tying up and sexually assaulting a 14-year-old boy at an apartment in Bukit Kemuning, Section 32, will be charged in court soon. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said police had received instructions from the Deputy Public Prosecutor's office to proceed with charges against the suspect. Shah Alam police chief Ramsay Embol previously said checks showed the suspect had also been convicted of rape, wrongful confinement and robbery involving a woman 18 years ago.

items.title: 4 arrested over cable theft in viral Semenyih video
items.summary: KAJANG: Police have arrested 4 suspects, including a woman, after a viral video showed a cable theft incident in Semenyih yesterday. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said the 41-second clip showed four people allegedly stealing cables using two cars along Jalan Zahra 1, Semenyih, about 5.45am on May 20. Naazron said checks showed the first suspect had a record involving 24 criminal offences and 10 drug-related offences, while the second had 10 criminal offences and four drug-related offences.

items.title: ‘Everything remains under control’: Penang’s essential goods supply stable with no sharp price increases, says CM
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 21 — The supply of essential goods in Penang is stable with no significant hike in prices, according to chief minister Chow Kon Yeow. “The 27 price-controlled items are in sufficient supply and are being sold below the controlled price,” he said in a press conference after visiting a local fruits and vegetables campaign at Komtar today. Everything remains under control,” he said.

items.title: Former Sungai Rengit police chief, Larkin station constable, charged with accepting bribes
items.summary: Inspector Ihsan Zaidani Hassan, 43, and Constable Muhammad Jefri Din, 25, pleaded not guilty before judge Mohd Azhar Othman. According to the three charges against Ihsan Zaidani, the then Sungai Rengit police station chief allegedly agreed to accept RM8,000 without consideration from a 70-year-old man through a 49-year-old middleman into his personal bank account. Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) prosecuting officer Alice Voon Mei Ling proposed bail at RM10,000 with one surety.

items.title: Bumi Armada 1Q net profit falls to RM40.11mil on weaker FPSO contributions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bumi Armada Bhd posted a lower net profit of RM40.11 million in the first quarter (1Q) ended March 31, 2026, compared with RM182.76 million a year earlier. The weaker performance was mainly due to lower contributions from Armada Kraken floating production, storage and offloading (FPSO) and Armada Olombendo FPSO, partly offset by lower finance costs. "The operations segment's revenue declined mainly due to lower revenue from Armada Kraken FPSO following the commencement of the annual option period charter in April 2025 at a lower charter rate, as well as lower finance lease revenue from Armada Olombendo FPSO, it said.

items.title: Tony Pua faces 59 police reports nationwide over allegedly sensitive Facebook remarks on monarchy, Rukun Negara
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — A total of 59 police reports have been lodged nationwide against former Damansara MP Tony Pua following a Facebook post alleged to have raised sensitive issues involving Malaysia’s constitutional monarchy and the Rukun Negara. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the investigation will be carried out by Selangor police, and Pua will be called in soon, according to Bernama. “As of this morning, I was briefed that 59 reports have been filed nationwide, with investigations led by the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) Selangor,” he told reporters at a press conference at the Selangor police headquarters today.

items.title: Too few Malaysians getting flu shots despite high awareness, doctors warn ahead of mid-year influenza season
items.summary: The call comes ahead of the country’s mid-year influenza season, with medical groups cautioning that older adults and those with chronic diseases remain vulnerable to severe complications from influenza infections. Of that group, only about half said they received the influenza shot annually. The survey was conducted collaboratively by the Health Ministry, the Malaysian Influenza Working Group (MIWG), and several public universities.

items.title: Babysitter remanded six days over death of seven‑month‑old boy in Seremban
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 21 — A babysitter detained to assist investigations into the death of a seven-month-old baby boy at a house in Bandar Sri Sendayan near here last Tuesday has been remanded for six days until May 25. Meanwhile, Negeri Sembilan Social Welfare Department (JKM) director Muhammad Yusry Che Dah said investigations found that the childcare centre was unregistered and operating without approval under the Child Care Centre Act 1984. Most cases involving the death or injury of children occur at unregistered babysitting homes and childcare centres,” he said.

items.title: Motorcyclist trapped and killed after rear‑ending excavator at Federal Highway exit in Shah Alam
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — A motorcyclist was killed after being trapped beneath an excavator in a road accident at the Federal Highway exit towards the junction of a sugar factory at midnight last night. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mokhtar said a team of five firemen in an engine from the Shah Alam station were deployed to the scene after they received a call at 12.06 am. “The victim, who was trapped beneath the excavator, was pronounced dead by Health Ministry personnel.

items.title: Motorcyclist dies at scene after suspected collision with cow in Tapah
items.summary: Tapah police chief Supt Abdul Malik Hashim said initial investigations found that the victim, a man in his 20s, was riding alone and was on his way home to Sungai Manik after work when the incident occurred. “Investigations at the scene found traces of cow dung and skid marks on the road. Abdul Malik said the case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.

items.title: Penang police bust ‘Piau Piau’ vape‑drug syndicate, arrest two and seize RM199,000 in drugs and assets
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 21 — Drugs packed into vape cartridges and sold on the streets as “Piau Piau” have resurfaced in Penang, after police busted a syndicate and seized almost RM200,000 worth of drugs and assets. He said the syndicate was found to be distributing a mix of methamphetamine liquid, MDMA powder, ketamine and Erimin 5 pills, with drugs concealed in vape‑style cartridges. He said the seizure included 123 cartridges suspected to contain methamphetamine liquid weighing 1,348.36 g, 11 packets of MDMA powder weighing 396 g, three packets of ketamine weighing 2.55 g, and 70 Erimin 5 pills weighing 21.37 g, alongside five electronic devices.

items.title: Global crisis driving push to reinforce Malaysia’s economic structure, says Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 21 — The government views the current global crisis as an impetus to strengthen the country’s economic structure, including a greater focus on high-value industries, the energy transition and a more inclusive approach to delivering public assistance. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said that, unlike the Covid-19 pandemic, which required stimulus spending due to the shutdown of economic activity, the current crisis began with pressure on oil supply before spreading to other sectors, resulting in a more complex economic impact. He said the situation has prompted the government to focus on strengthening the country’s economic resilience through more sustainable structural changes.

items.title: Govt studying shift from B40–T20 income groups to PAKW‑based assessment for fairer aid targeting, says Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 21 — The Ministry of Economy is studying a fairer and more comprehensive approach in determining eligibility for public aid and subsidies by taking into account net disposable income and the actual commitments of households, instead of relying solely on the existing B40, M40 and T20 classifications. “We are bringing forward discussions on how to assess people’s living capacity not solely based on income. The income earned must take into account the basic expenditures needed for an individual or household to sustain a reasonable standard of living,” he said.

items.title: In Aidiladha message, Selangor Sultan says Malay unity should not be misconstrued, mutual respect strengthens national stability
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, has stressed that calls for Malay unity should not be misconstrued as hostility towards other races in Malaysia. Instead, His Royal Highness said united communities would contribute to a more peaceful and stable nation, while fostering mutual respect among the people. In the royal message, Sultan Sharafuddin and the Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor, Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin, also extended Aidiladha greetings to Muslims nationwide, especially those in Selangor.

items.title: Johor PAS dismisses claims it will give way to BN in state election, says party will contest state polls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 21 — Johor PAS today clarified that it is prepared to contest in the coming state election, contrary to a social media post claiming that it will withdraw to make way for Barisan Nasional (BN). Johor PAS information chief Mohd Firdaus Jaffar said the viral social media post was made by irresponsible parties to create a negative perception and weaken the party’s machinery. Mohd Firdaus was responding to recent social media claims saying Johor PAS is prepared to fully give way to BN’s linchpin Umno to contest in the coming state elections.

items.title: MB: Terengganu rolls out Phase 1 economic plan to support MSMEs and farmers amid global supply crisis
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, May 21 — The Terengganu state government today announced Phase 1 of its State Economic Stimulus Action Plan to address the global supply crisis. “This initiative is expected to benefit 783 borrowers, involving uncollected loan repayments amounting to RM2.1 million. Ahmad Samsuri said a moratorium will also be given to about 400 borrowers under the Agricultural Capital Assistance Scheme from this month until April 2027, involving uncollected loan repayments amounting to RM2.9 million.

items.title: Two men killed after riverbank embankment collapses during suspected gold mining in Jeli
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — Two men were killed when a two‑metre riverbank embankment collapsed while they were believed to be mining for gold at a stream in the Jerimbong oil palm plantation in Jeli last night. Jeli Fire and Rescue Station chief Adni Ibrahim said his team received an emergency call about victims buried under soil before firefighters were deployed to the scene. He said the search began at 9.20pm and firefighters confirmed that two victims were buried under the collapsed embankment.

items.title: Tonda Pizza Romana duped by ‘raw’ pizza scammer with ‘AI‑magic’ photo on GrabFood (VIDEO)
items.summary: Alan Goh, the owner of Tonda Pizza Romana in Taman Desa, found himself the unwitting star of an unusual complaint that could only be described in one way: “when AI meets pizza.” The trouble began when an unhappy customer lodged a complaint through GrabFood, claiming their pizza had arrived completely raw. “Hello, I received a raw pizza with my order. “The dough and the ingredients looked completely raw,” said the 35-year-old restaurant owner.

items.title: Uncle tells inquest Zara Qairina, an only child, had close bond with her mother
items.summary: The deceased’s uncle, Abu Bakar Lamat, 47, said he had also never seen Zara Qairina suffer from serious family-related stress, adding that the teenager mostly shared stories about school and her friends. The 72nd witness said Zara Qairina had a very close relationship with her mother, as she was an only child, and frequently communicated with her mother through phone calls while staying at the hostel. He said the deceased’s mother had also never neglected or treated Zara Qairina in an inappropriate manner.

items.title: Sabah floods: 338 evacuees in Beaufort as of 8am
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 21 — The number of flood evauees in Beaufort remained largely unchanged at 338 people from 121 families as of 8am today, compared with 336 people from 119 families recorded at 8pm yesterday. The Sabah Disaster Management Committee Secretariat, in a statement, said that all evacuees are currently being housed at the Selagon permanent relief centre (PPK), which was opened at 3pm last Tuesday (May 19). “The number of villages affected by the floods increased to 11 this morning, compared with 10 villages last night.

items.title: MOF: RON97 up 15 sen, unsubsidised RON95 up 20 sen, diesel up 10 sen for May 21–27
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — The retail prices of RON97, unsubsidised RON95 and diesel in Peninsular Malaysia will increase by 15 sen, 20 sen and 10 sen per litre respectively for the period from May 21 to 27. The Ministry of Finance (MOF) said in a statement yesterday that the retail price of RON97 would be set at RM4.85 per litre compared with RM4.70 previously, while unsubsidised RON95 would rise to RM4.07 per litre from RM3.87 previously. The price of diesel in Peninsular Malaysia will meanwhile be set at RM4.97 per litre compared with RM4.87 previously.

items.title: Johor Royal Press Office urges stern action against fake TikTok account insulting King
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — The Johor Royal Press Office has called for stern action to be taken against those responsible for creating a fake TikTok account to slander and insult His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia. According to a post on the King’s official Facebook page, the Royal Press Office said the account, using the name “Sultan Ibrahim Ismail”, was found to be using Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology to spread insulting content against His Majesty. “The Royal Press Office also expresses regret over another image in the account which depicted His Majesty’s face superimposed onto the body of an animal, which was clearly insulting and irresponsible,” the statement said.

items.title: Police open probe into Tony Pua over alleged sensitive social media remarks on monarchy, Rukun Negara
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — Police have opened an investigation paper against former Damansara Member of Parliament Tony Pua following a statement uploaded on his Facebook account that allegedly touched on sensitive issues involving the Constitutional Monarchy institution and the Rukun Negara. “The investigation was initiated after police received 28 reports nationwide regarding the post,” he said briefly when contacted here yesterday. He said the police would summon all parties involved, including Tony, soon to assist in the investigation.

items.title: MACC probing two individuals over IJM Corp share acquisition
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — Two investigation papers involving the voluntary acquisition of IJM Corporation by Sunway Berhad remain open and active following the discovery of elements related to alleged irregularities in the matter. Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Abd Halim Aman said investigations found elements that could be investigated under the MACC Act 2009 as well as the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (AMLA), involving two individuals. He said that so far, the MACC had recorded statements from 13 individuals to assist in the investigation.

items.title: RM1.1b investment probe: MACC seeks AGC help to get statements from Taiwan, UK in case linked to Arm Holdings
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 21 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is seeking mutual legal assistance to obtain statements from several individuals in Taiwan and the United Kingdom (UK) to assist in the investigation into the RM1.1 billion investment case. MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said his party had contacted the Attorney General’s Chambers to seek assistance following the investigation which also involved the jurisdictions of the two countries involved. So the investigation may take time,” he told Bernama after a working visit to the Selangor MACC office here today.

items.title: Anwar calls on Malaysian envoys to boost economic diplomacy, attract quality investments
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 21 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim wants Malaysian diplomats to continue strengthening the nation’s economic diplomacy capabilities, particularly in attracting trade, high-quality investments and exploring strategic cooperation opportunities capable of empowering sustainable national economic growth. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, emphasised the matter when receiving courtesy calls from 13 Malaysian Heads of Diplomatic Missions who will soon be serving in various strategic regions around the world yesterday. Anwar said Malaysia would continue to uphold the principles of moderation, stability, good governance as well as a diplomacy approach grounded in wisdom and universal humanitarian values in carrying the nation’s voice on the world stage.

items.title: EC: No by‑elections for Pandan, Setiawangsa seats after vacancies declared
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 21 — Election Commission (EC) chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun has confirmed that no by-elections will be held for the Pandan and Setiawangsa parliamentary seats. He said the EC received notification from Johari through a letter dated today regarding the status of the two parliamentary seats after they were declared vacant under Article 51 of the Federal Constitution. On Sunday (May 17), Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli, who is also a former PKR deputy president, and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, a former PKR vice-president, announced that they would vacate their parliamentary seats on Monday and leave the party effective Tuesday through letters submitted to the party secretary-general.
